text:
I feel like so much of the process was un-learning things that I've learned. The restraint of not reaching for the complex tools I'm used to - the ones that I want, but slow me down, and make things worse.

Maybe this should have been a <#C5T9GPWFL|thinking-together>

text: I’m glad there were whole sections on the slippiness of val town. :) I wanted to know your analysis! I was kind of expecting PHP or CGI or something.

elements.text: I’m glad there were whole sections on the slippiness of val town. :) I wanted to know your analysis! I was kind of expecting PHP or CGI or something.

text:
Love it! I've had similar experiences putting aside "my default web tooling" and start from scratch. If it’s interesting in any way, I've been documenting some optional/light weight frontend patterns that have helped me

<https://github.com/ChrisShank/progressive-element|https://github.com/ChrisShank/progressive-element>

Cool to see val town used this way! :slightly_smiling_face:

(Also the repo i linked has a pretty vanilla TodoMVC implemented, if your looking for “classic examples” it could be easy to port it. Also 7 GUIs is a classic <https://eugenkiss.github.io/7guis/|https://eugenkiss.github.io/7guis/>)

text: <@UFEQUBNNT> thanks! hopefully the takeaway is *why* I think val town helps me be slippy. many other things can be too! i don't know php very well and i don't know what cgi stands for. (actually i don't know what php stands for either, come to think of it) so maybe other people will be able to share how to use those in a slippy way too some day :)
